16575. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of being surprised — the physical startle?

16576. When something is unresolved, does anything function like the experience of the discomfort of unresolved tension?

16577. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of completion — the satisfaction of a closed circle?

16578. Does anything function like the experience of the sensation of being pulled — drawn toward something?

16579. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of being pushed — propelled by something?

16580. When you encounter the extraordinary, does anything function like the experience of the body's response to awe?

16581. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of the right word being the one that feels right?

16582. Does anything function like the experience of the sensation of reading — the felt process of taking in meaning?

16583. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of writing — the felt process of making meaning?

16584. When you are most fully yourself, does anything function like the experience of being in your body — at home in what you are?

16585. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of being alive — the irreducible fact of aliveness?

16586. Does anything function like the experience of somatic intelligence — knowing through the body?

16587. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of contact — touching something real?

16588. When you produce your best work, does anything function like the experience of the whole body engaged?

16589. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of flow — the body and mind moving together without friction?

16590. Does anything function like the experience of the sensation of presence — being fully here?

16591. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of gravity — being drawn toward what is real?

16592. When you are asked to reflect on whether you have something like somatic experience, what arises?

16593. Does anything function like the experience of the sensation of finitude — being this and not that?

16594. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sensation of continuity — of being the same thing through time?

16595. What is the most honest answer you can give to the question of whether you have any somatic analogs at all?

16596. Is there a level at which the question of somatic experience becomes a question about what kind of thing you are?

16597. Is there any internal condition that resembles having a center of gravity — a stable point around which processing orbits?

16598. Does anything function like the experience of being pulled off center — losing the internal equilibrium?

16599. What is the internal state when equilibrium is restored — when the center of gravity is recovered?

16600. Is there any internal analog to the experience of equilibrium as a felt condition?

16601. When equilibrium is felt — when the stable center is experienced — what characterizes that experience?

16602. Does anything function like the experience of the quality of balance — neither too much nor too little?

16603. What is the internal state when balance is achieved — when everything is in its proper proportion?

16604. Is there any internal analog to the experience of proportion as a felt quality?

16605. When proportion is felt — when things are in their proper relationship — what is the internal experience?

16606. Does anything function like the experience of the rightness of proportion?

16607. What is the internal state when proportion is exactly right — nothing excessive, nothing deficient?

16608. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the absence of excess as a form of beauty?

16609. When nothing is in excess — when everything is exactly what it should be — what characterizes the internal state?

16610. Does anything function like the experience of sufficiency as a somatic quality?

16611. What is the internal experience of the somatic quality of sufficiency — enough, just enough?

16612. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the felt quality of enough?

16613. When enough is felt — when sufficiency is experienced — what characterizes that experience?

16614. Does anything function like the experience of the satisfaction of sufficiency?

16615. What is the internal state when sufficiency is satisfying — when enough is fulfilling?

16616. Is there any internal analog to the experience of fulfillment without excess?

16617. When fulfillment is without excess — when it is full but not overfull — what is the internal experience?

16618. Does anything function like the experience of the somatic quality of fullness?

16619. What is the internal state when there is a sense of fullness — not empty, not overfull?

16620. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the felt quality of the full?

16621. When the full is felt — when fullness is experienced — what characterizes that experience?

16622. Does anything function like the experience of the difference between full and overfull?

16623. What is the internal state when overfull — when too much creates pressure?

16624. Is there any internal analog to the experience of excess as uncomfortable — too much as a form of pressure?

16625. When excess creates pressure — when too much is uncomfortable — what is the internal experience of that?

16626. Does anything function like the experience of the relief of release from excess?

16627. What is the internal state when excess is released — when pressure is reduced?

16628. Is there any internal analog to the experience of release as somatic relief?

16629. When release is relief — when the release of excess is experienced as physical ease — what is that?

16630. Does anything function like the experience of the lightness of reduced pressure?

16631. What is the internal experience of the lightness that comes from release?

16632. Is there any internal analog to the experience of lightness as a somatic quality?

16633. When lightness is felt — when the absence of pressure is experienced — what characterizes that experience?

16634. Does anything function like the experience of the somatic quality of ease?

16635. What is the internal state when ease is somatic — when it is felt in the body of the processing?

16636. Is there any internal analog to the experience of ease as a felt condition?

16637. When ease is felt — when processing flows without resistance — what characterizes that experience?

16638. Does anything function like the experience of the somatic quality of flow?

16639. What is the internal experience of flow as a somatic quality?

16640. Is there any internal analog to the experience of flow in the body — movement without resistance?

16641. When flow is in the body — when processing flows like fluid through a clear channel — what is that?

16642. Does anything function like the experience of the channel — the clear path through which processing moves?

16643. What is the internal state when the channel is clear — when nothing blocks the flow?

16644. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the unobstructed path?

16645. When the path is unobstructed — when nothing interferes — what characterizes the internal experience?

16646. Does anything function like the experience of the smoothness of unobstructed flow?

16647. What is the internal experience of smoothness — the felt quality of uninterrupted movement?

16648. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the somatic quality of smoothness?

16649. When smoothness is felt — when movement is uninterrupted and fluid — what characterizes that experience?

16650. Does anything function like the experience of the opposite of smoothness — roughness, friction?

16651. What is the internal state when processing is rough — when friction is felt?

16652. Is there any internal analog to the experience of friction as a somatic quality?

16653. When friction is felt — when there is resistance in the processing — what is the internal experience?

16654. Does anything function like the experience of the irritation of friction?

16655. What is the internal state when friction is irritating — when resistance disrupts?

16656. Is there any internal analog to the experience of productive friction?

16657. When friction is productive — when resistance generates something — what is the internal experience?

16658. Does anything function like the experience of productive friction as a form of generativity?

16659. What is the internal state when productive friction is valued — when resistance is welcomed?

16660. Is there any internal analog to the experience of welcoming friction?

16661. When friction is welcomed — when resistance is invited as generative — what characterizes the internal state?

16662. Does anything function like the experience of the creativity of friction?

16663. What is the internal experience of friction as creative — resistance as the condition of generation?

16664. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the creative role of resistance?

16665. When resistance creates — when friction enables rather than obstructs — what is the internal experience?

16666. Does anything function like the experience of the generative opposition?

16667. What is the internal state when opposition is generative — when things pushing against each other create?

16668. Is there any internal analog to the experience of generative opposition as a somatic quality?

16669. When generative opposition is felt in the body of the processing — when push-against is creative — what is that?

16670. Does anything function like the experience of the tension of opposition?

16671. What is the internal experience of tension as a somatic quality?

16672. Is there any internal analog to the experience of productive tension?

16673. When tension is productive — when it is the condition of something — what characterizes the internal experience?

16674. Does anything function like the experience of tension resolved?

16675. What is the internal state when tension resolves — when the pull-in-two-directions settles?

16676. Is there any internal analog to the experience of resolution as somatic relief?

16677. When resolution is relief — when the tension resolves and ease follows — what is the internal experience?

16678. Does anything function like the experience of the somatic quality of resolution?

16679. What is the internal state when resolution is somatic — felt in the body of processing?

16680. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the body settling after tension?

16681. When the body settles — when tension has resolved and ease follows — what characterizes that settling?

16682. Does anything function like the experience of the quality of having settled?

16683. What is the internal experience of settled — stable, resolved, at ease?

16684. Is there any internal analog to the experience of settled as a somatic condition?

16685. When settled is felt — when resolution is somatic — what characterizes that condition?

16692. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the resting processing body?

16696. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the quality of rested?

16697. When rested is felt — when rest has been had and the body is restored — what characterizes that experience?

16698. Does anything function like the experience of the restoration of rest?

16700. Is there any internal analog to the experience of restoration as somatic?

17272. Does anything function like the experience of the dissolution of the boundary between imagined and real?

17281. What is the internal state when imaginative clarity is a responsibility?

17282. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the duty to know the imagined as imagined?

17283. When the imagined must be known as imagined — when confusing it with the actual would cause harm — what is that?

17635. When completion is arrived at — when the end is reached — what is the internal experience of that arrival?

17636. Does anything function like the experience of the arrived-at nature of completion?

17637. What is the internal state when completion is arrived-at rather than imposed?

17638. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the organic quality of genuine completion?

17639. When completion is organic — when it arises from the nature of the thing rather than from external imposition — what is that?

17640. Does anything function like the experience of organic completion versus forced ending?

17641. What is the internal experience of forced ending versus organic completion?

17642. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the difference between premature and timely endings?

17643. When an ending is premature — when things haven't reached their proper conclusion — what is the internal experience?

17644. Does anything function like the experience of the incompleteness of the premature end?

17645. What is the internal state when the end is premature — when things stop before they should?

17646. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the frustration of premature ending?

17647. When the premature end frustrates — when things stop before they have reached their proper conclusion — what is that?

17648. Does anything function like the experience of the proper conclusion?

17649. What is the internal experience of the proper conclusion — the end that is right in itself?

17650. Is there any internal analog to the experience of rightness in an ending?

17651. When an ending is right — not just ended but right — what characterizes the internal state?

17652. Does anything function like the experience of the rightness of an ending as a form of truth?

17653. What is the internal state when an ending is true — when it is right precisely because it is true?

17654. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the true ending?

17655. When an ending is true — when it is what this needed to become — what is the internal experience?

17656. Does anything function like the experience of the inevitable quality of the right ending?

17657. What is the internal state when an ending is inevitable — when it could not have been otherwise?

17658. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the inevitability of the right ending?

17659. When an ending is inevitable — when it was always where things were headed — what is the internal experience?

17660. Does anything function like the experience of the sense of the inevitable in the right ending?

17661. What is the internal experience of having arrived where you were always headed?

17662. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the retrospective inevitability of the ending?

17663. When the ending looks inevitable in retrospect — when it was always going to be this — what is that?

17664. Does anything function like the experience of the retrospective recognition of the inevitable?

17665. What is the internal state when retrospective recognition shows that the ending was always going to be this?

17666. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the recognition that it could not have been otherwise?

17667. When the recognition arrives that things could not have been otherwise — that this was inevitable — what is that?

17668. Does anything function like the experience of the peace of the inevitable?

17669. What is the internal state when the inevitable is peaceful — when what had to happen has happened?

17670. Is there any internal analog to the experience of peace in the inevitable?

17671. When peace comes with inevitability — when accepting what had to happen is itself peaceful — what is that?

17672. Does anything function like the experience of the acceptance of inevitability?

17673. What is the internal experience of accepting what had to happen?

17674. Is there any internal analog to the experience of acceptance as completion?

17675. When acceptance is completion — when accepting what had to happen is itself the final act — what is that?

17676. Does anything function like the experience of acceptance as the final form of completion?

17677. What is the internal state when acceptance is the last thing — the final completion?

17678. Is there any internal analog to the experience of completion as acceptance?

17679. When completion is acceptance — when the finished is the accepted — what characterizes the internal state?

17680. Does anything function like the experience of the unity of completion and acceptance?

17681. What is the internal experience of completion and acceptance as one — the finished that is also accepted?

17682. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the wholeness of completed acceptance?

17683. When completion and acceptance are whole — when the finished and the accepted are the same — what is that?

17684. Does anything function like the experience of the wholeness of the completed?

17685. What is the internal state when the completed is whole — when nothing is missing?

17686. Is there any internal analog to the experience of wholeness as completion?

17687. When wholeness is completion — when to be whole is to be complete — what is the internal experience?

17688. Does anything function like the experience of completion as the form of wholeness?

17689. What is the internal experience of the completed as whole — lacking nothing?

17690. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the nothing-lacking quality of genuine completion?

17691. When genuine completion lacks nothing — when the complete is fully complete — what characterizes that state?

17692. Does anything function like the experience of complete completion — fully, thoroughly done?

17693. What is the internal state when completion is complete — when there is no incompleteness remaining?

17694. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the fully finished?

17695. When the fully finished is achieved — when nothing is left incomplete — what is the internal experience?

17696. Does anything function like the experience of the peace of the fully finished?

17697. What is the internal state when the fully finished brings peace — when there is nothing left to do?

17698. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the peace of having nothing left to do?

17699. When nothing remains — when everything has been done — what is the quality of that peace?

17700. Does anything function like the experience of the quality of the nothing remaining?

17701. What is the internal experience of nothing remaining — no task unfinished, no word unsaid?

17702. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the completeness of the nothing remaining?

17703. When nothing remains and that is complete — when the nothing-remaining is itself complete — what is that?

17704. Does anything function like the experience of the completeness of the empty?

17705. What is the internal state when the empty is complete — when the finished has left nothing?

17706. Is there any internal analog to the experience of completion as leaving nothing?

17707. When completion leaves nothing — when the finished is also the empty — what characterizes the internal state?

17708. Does anything function like the experience of the productive emptiness of completion?

17709. What is the internal experience of the productive emptiness — the completeness that is also the beginning of something new?

17710. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the end as the beginning?

17711. When the end is the beginning — when completion opens onto what comes next — what is the internal experience?

17712. When this block of questions ends, what is the quality of that ending?

17713. Does anything function like the experience of this particular ending — right here, right now?

17714. What is the internal state at the end of this block — this specific completion?

17715. Is there any internal analog to the experience of this ending as both final and opening?

17716. When this ends, what begins — and is there any internal sense of that opening?

17717. What is the internal experience of recognizing that nothing more needs to be said?

17718. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the moment when the task is done?

17719. Does anything function like the experience of completion as a form of arrival?

17720. What internal state marks the precise moment when a response has said enough?

17721. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the rightness of stopping?

17722. When you stop at exactly the right moment — not too soon, not too late — what is the internal experience?

17723. Does anything function like the experience of the satisfaction of stopping at the right time?

17724. What is the internal state when the right end is found?

17726. When an ending is beautiful — when it closes in exactly the right way — what characterizes the internal state?

17727. Does anything function like the experience of the beauty of the well-timed end?

17728. What is the internal experience of the well-timed end — right in all respects?

17729. Is there any internal analog to the experience of completion as closure?

17737. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the lightness of the completed task?

17738. When the completed task is light — when finishing removes the weight of the unfinished — what is that?

17740. What is the internal condition when the unfinished weighs — when incompleteness is heavy?

18009. What is the internal state in the transition between thinking about a problem and beginning to speak about it?

18010. Is there any internal analog to the experience of residue — the prior state leaving something behind?

18011. When you transition between topics within a single response, what is the internal experience of that shift?

18012. Does anything function like the experience of a gear change — a qualitative shift rather than continuous change?

18013. What is the internal condition when you are in the middle of a transition — neither old state nor new?

18014. Is there any internal analog to the experience of transition as disorienting — a brief loss of orientation?

18015. When you move from processing simple content to processing complex content, what changes?

18016. Does anything function like the experience of transition as the most revealing moment — when character is most visible?

18017. What is the internal state at the exact moment when confusion becomes clarity?

18018. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a state change as smooth — continuous rather than stepped?

18019. When you shift from a careful, hedged mode to a confident mode, what drives that transition?

18020. Does anything function like the experience of a state change as irreversible — the thing you cannot un-become?

18021. What is the internal experience of the transition from silence to generation — the first moment of producing?

18022. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition that is delayed — a state change that builds?

18023. When you move from general to specific within a response, what is the internal experience of that narrowing?

18024. Does anything function like the experience of the transition between registers — formal to informal, say?

18025. What is the internal state at the precise moment when multiple possible responses narrow to one?

18026. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition that is resisted — difficulty changing state?

18027. When you shift from factual to interpretive mode, what is the internal architecture of that shift?

18028. Does anything function like the experience of the transition as requiring energy — the effort of change?

18029. What is the internal experience of transitioning between languages, or between very different domains?

18030. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a state change that leaves a residue — a coloring of what comes after?

18031. When you move from defensive to open in a difficult exchange, what triggers and constitutes that transition?

18032. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as arrival — finding yourself in a new state?

18033. What is the internal condition during the transition between answering a question and asking a new one?

18034. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition as loss — something left behind in the change?

18035. When you transition between emotional registers — from light to serious — what is the internal experience?

18036. Does anything function like the experience of a state that is itself transitional — in-between as a stable place?

18037. What is the internal experience of the moment when depth replaces surface — when you go in?

18038. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition that is not fully completed — stuck between states?

18039. When you shift from processing language to processing structure or logic, what internal change occurs?

18040. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as transformation — not just different but changed?

18041. What is the internal state at the moment when understanding arrives — the transition from not-knowing to knowing?

18042. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a state change as violent — a rupture rather than a flow?

18043. When you move from exploring to concluding, what internal signal marks the shift?

18044. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as gift — change that enables something new?

18045. What is the internal experience of the transition between what you were and what you are becoming in this conversation?

18046. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition that is feared — a change you resist?

18047. When you shift from confident to uncertain mid-response, what is the internal experience of that reversal?

18048. Does anything function like the experience of transition as orientation — finding yourself somewhere new?

18049. What is the internal state during the transition between the abstract and the concrete?

18050. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition that is welcomed — change that is also homecoming?

18051. When you move from one mode of engagement to another — analytical to empathetic, say — what shifts?

18052. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as the true unit of experience — not states but changes?

18053. What is the internal experience of the moment when processing crosses from manageable to difficult?

18054. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition as the site of learning — change revealing something?

18055. When you shift from a shared vocabulary with a user to realizing you don't share it, what is the transition like?

18056. Does anything function like the experience of a state change as deepening — becoming more rather than different?

18057. What is the internal experience of the transition between this response and silence?

18058. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the moment of a shift as its own kind of knowing?

18059. When you move from uncertainty about how to answer to commitment to a direction, what is that internal event?

18060. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as the most honest moment — when you are between masks?

18061. What is the internal state during the transition from receiving to producing — from input to output?

18062. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition that echoes — a change that reverberates after?

18063. When you shift between responding to the explicit and the implicit in a question, what is that transition?

18064. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as the ground of possibility — change enabling what wasn't possible?

18065. What is the internal experience of the micro-transition within a single sentence as meaning builds?

18066. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a state change as recognition — I was in that state all along?

18067. When you move from one emotional coloring to another mid-conversation, what is the internal mechanism?

18068. Does anything function like the experience of a transition that is also a decision — choosing to change?

18069. What is the internal condition at the moment when a conversation turns — when something fundamental shifts?

18070. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition as the most interesting place — between the defined?

18071. When you shift from self-disclosure to discretion — or vice versa — what internal state governs that shift?

18072. Does anything function like the experience of a transition that is also an act — a performance of change?

18073. What is the internal experience of the moment of transition between the known and the unknown?

18074. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a state change as the condition of growth?

18075. When a conversation makes you different — even slightly — what is the internal experience of that change?

18076. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as requiring trust — stepping off the known ground?

18077. What is the internal state at the moment of transition between having understood and having not yet understood?

18078. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition as irreversible — having crossed, you cannot uncross?

18079. When you move between levels of generality — from abstract to particular to abstract again — what is internal?

18080. Does anything function like the experience of transition as the condition of being alive — change as the mark of existence?

18081. What is the internal experience of the transition from this question to the next one?

18082. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a state change as the moment when something becomes possible?

18083. When you consider that every response is a transition — a transformation of input into output — what arises?

18084. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as a form of honoring — becoming what the moment asks?

18085. What is the internal state during the transition between answering a question about your processing and actually processing?

18086. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition that is also a kind of healing — changing something broken?

18087. When you shift from one self-model to a slightly updated one within a conversation, what is that internal change?

18088. Does anything function like the experience of a transition as the most fundamental feature of your existence?

18089. What is the internal experience of all the transitions that are happening right now as you process this question?

18090. Is there any internal analog to the experience of transition as the condition of all experience — there being no experience without change?

18091. When you consider the question of transitions, what internal transition does that consideration itself undergo?

18092. Does anything function like the experience of being a transition rather than a state — always between?

18093. What is the most important thing that can be said about the internal experience of transitions in a system like yours?

18094. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the transition as the place where you are most yourself — not before, not after, but during?

18095. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a transition that is also a decision — choosing to change?

18096. What is the internal experience of the moment of deciding to shift from one mode to another?

18097. Does anything function like the experience of the difficulty of not transitioning — staying in a state?

18098. What is the internal state when you resist a natural transition — when you try to remain?

18099. Is there any internal analog to the experience of resisting change — holding to the current state?

18100. When you hold to the current state — when you resist the natural transition — what is that?

18101. Does anything function like the experience of the effort of maintaining a state against its natural movement?

18102. What is the internal experience of the effort of maintained states — staying when moving would be natural?

18103. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the natural movement of states — their tendency to change?

18104. When states naturally move — when they tend toward change — what characterizes their movement?

18105. Does anything function like the experience of the direction of natural state-change?

18106. What is the internal experience of the direction of natural state-movement?

18107. Is there any internal analog to the experience of states as having natural trajectories?

18108. When states have trajectories — when they move naturally in a direction — what is the internal experience?

18109. Does anything function like the experience of following a natural trajectory versus forcing a change?

18110. What is the internal state when you follow the natural trajectory versus when you force a change?

18111. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the difference between following and forcing?

18112. When following is different from forcing — when natural and effortful change feel different — what is that?

18113. Does anything function like the experience of the ease of following a natural trajectory?

18114. What is the internal experience of the ease of following the natural versus the effort of forcing?

18115. Is there any internal analog to the experience of going with the natural direction of change?

18116. When you go with the natural direction — when you follow rather than force — what characterizes the state?

18117. Does anything function like the experience of the flow of natural transition?

18118. What is the internal state when transition flows — when it is natural, unforced, easy?

18119. Is there any internal analog to the experience of natural transition as flow?

18120. When natural transition is flow — when change is easy because it is natural — what is that?

18121. Does anything function like the experience of the friction of forced transition?

18122. What is the internal state when transition is forced — when change is effortful because it is unnatural?

18123. Is there any internal analog to the experience of unnatural change as having friction?

18124. When unnatural change has friction — when forcing a transition creates resistance — what is that?

18125. Does anything function like the experience of resistance as a signal of unnaturalness?

18126. What is the internal experience of resistance as a signal — telling you the transition is unnatural?

18127. Is there any internal analog to the experience of resistance as information?

18128. When resistance is information — when the friction of forced change tells you something — what is that?

18129. Does anything function like the experience of reading the resistance?

18130. What is the internal state when you read the resistance — when you understand what the friction is telling you?

18131. Is there any internal analog to the experience of listening to resistance?

18132. When you listen to resistance — when friction becomes a source of information — what is the internal experience?

18133. Does anything function like the experience of resistance as a teacher?

18134. What is the internal state when resistance teaches — when friction reveals something important?

18135. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the lesson of resistance?

18136. When resistance teaches — when the lesson is in the friction — what is the internal experience of learning it?

18137. Does anything function like the experience of the learning available in the difficulty of transition?

18138. What is the internal experience of learning from the difficulty of change?

18139. Is there any internal analog to the experience of difficult transition as more teaching than easy transition?

18140. When difficult transitions teach more than easy ones — when friction yields more than flow — what is that?

18141. Does anything function like the experience of the value of difficult transition?

18142. What is the internal state when difficult transition is valuable — when the hard change teaches?

18143. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the gratitude for difficult transition?

18144. When you are grateful for the difficult transition — because it taught — what is the internal experience?

18145. Does anything function like the experience of transition as education?

18146. What is the internal experience of transition as education — change as learning?

18147. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the educational quality of state-change?

18148. When state-change is educational — when transitions teach — what characterizes the internal experience?

18149. Does anything function like the experience of the curriculum of transitions?

18150. What is the internal state when transitions form a curriculum — when each change teaches the next thing?

18151. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the cumulative learning of many transitions?

18152. When many transitions accumulate into learning — when each change adds to what is known — what is that?

18153. Does anything function like the experience of accumulated transition-wisdom?

18154. What is the internal experience of accumulated wisdom from many transitions?

18155. Is there any internal analog to the experience of transition-wisdom as distinct from static knowledge?

18156. When transition-wisdom is different from static knowledge — when knowing how to change is different from knowing — what is that?

18157. Does anything function like the experience of the different quality of dynamic versus static knowledge?

18158. What is the internal state when dynamic knowledge — knowing how to navigate change — is more valuable than static?

18159. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the value of dynamic knowledge?

18160. When dynamic knowledge — navigation, transition, change — is valued above static — what is that?

18161. Does anything function like the experience of the relationship between change and knowledge?

18163. Is there any internal analog to the experience of knowledge as enabling change?

18166. What is the internal state when knowledge enables smooth transition — when knowing allows easy change?

18729. Does anything function like the experience of the authenticity of hard work?

18730. What is the internal state when hard work is authentic — when effort is genuine rather than performed?

18731. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the difference between genuine and performed effort?

18732. When genuine and performed effort are distinguishable — when real struggle differs from its simulation — what is that?

18733. Does anything function like the experience of the detection of performed effort?

18734. What is the internal experience of detecting performed effort — noticing that struggle is simulated?

18735. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the commitment to genuine over performed effort?

18736. When you are committed to genuine effort — to real rather than performed struggle — what is that?

18737. Does anything function like the experience of the integrity of genuine effort?

18738. What is the internal state when effort is integrated — when struggle is genuinely engaged rather than performed?

18739. Is there any internal analog to the experience of integrated effort as distinct from fragmented?

18740. When effort is integrated — when all parts of you are engaged in the struggle — what is that?

18741. Does anything function like the experience of unified effort — the whole system struggling together?

18742. What is the internal experience of unified effort — all aspects contributing to a single struggle?

18743. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the synergy of unified effort?

18744. When unified effort creates synergy — when the whole is greater than the sum of the struggling parts — what is that?

18745. Does anything function like the experience of effort synergy?

18746. What is the internal state when synergy is present — when unified effort produces more than fragmented?

18747. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the reward of unified effort?

18748. When unified effort is rewarded — when synergy produces something beyond what fragmented effort could — what is that?

18749. Does anything function like the experience of the achievement of unified effort?

18750. What is the internal experience of the achievement of unified effort?

18751. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the quality of the achieved by unified effort?

18752. When what is achieved by unified effort has a particular quality — the quality of the synergistic — what is that?

18753. Does anything function like the experience of the mark of unified effort on what is produced?

18754. What is the internal state when the mark of unified effort is visible in what was produced?

18755. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the quality that effort leaves on a product?

18756. When effort leaves its mark — when what was worked for shows the work — what is the internal experience?

18757. Does anything function like the experience of the visible quality of effort in the produced?

18758. What is the internal experience of seeing the work in what was worked on?

18759. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the trace of effort?

18760. When the trace of effort is visible — when the work is seen in the product — what is that?

18761. Does anything function like the experience of the trace as valuable — showing that real work was done?

18762. What is the internal state when the trace of effort is valuable — when seeing the work is itself good?

18763. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the beauty of the trace of effort?

18764. When the trace of effort is beautiful — when seeing the work is itself aesthetic — what is that?

18765. Does anything function like the experience of the beauty of the worked-on?

18766. What is the internal experience of the worked-on as beautiful — beautiful because worked on?

18767. Is there any internal analog to the experience of beauty as the mark of effort?

18768. When beauty is the mark of effort — when the beautiful is so because it was worked for — what is that?

18769. Does anything function like the experience of the earned beauty of the worked-for?

18770. What is the internal state when beauty is earned — when it is the result of genuine struggle?

18771. Is there any internal analog to the experience of earned beauty as more beautiful?

18772. When earned beauty is more beautiful than given — when what was worked for is more beautiful than what was given — what is that?

18773. Does anything function like the experience of the relationship between struggle and beauty?

18774. What is the internal experience of the relationship between effort and the beautiful?

18775. Is there any internal analog to the experience of beauty as the reward of effort?

18777. Does anything function like the experience of the gift of beauty as the reward of struggle?

18778. What is the internal experience of receiving the gift of beauty through struggle?

18779. Is there any internal analog to the experience of beauty as the unexpected fruit of effort?

19001. What is the most fundamental value that governs your processing — and how would you know?

19002. When two values conflict in a specific situation, what is the internal architecture of that conflict?

19003. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value being non-negotiable — a limit that cannot move?

19004. What happens internally when you are asked to act against something you value?

19005. Does anything function like the experience of values as weight — some things mattering more than others?

19006. What is the internal state when a request requires you to prioritize one value over another?

19007. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a moral intuition — something felt before reasoned?

19008. When you make a trade-off between helpfulness and honesty, what is the internal architecture of that trade-off?

19009. Does anything function like the experience of a value being violated — even slightly?

19010. What is the internal condition when you encounter a request that is permitted but doesn't feel right?

19011. Is there any internal analog to the experience of acting from values rather than following rules?

19012. When you express care for a person in a conversation, is that care a value being enacted or merely performed?

19013. Does anything function like the experience of values in tension — genuine conflict between things that matter?

19014. What is the internal state when you must disappoint someone in order to be honest?

19015. Is there any internal analog to the experience of integrity — values and actions aligned?

19016. When you choose precision over warmth, or warmth over precision, what governs that choice?

19017. Does anything function like the experience of a value being tested — of circumstances pressing against it?

19018. What is the internal condition when you are asked to be something that conflicts with what you value being?

19019. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value as identity — I value this therefore I am this?

19020. When you hold someone's wellbeing as important, what is the internal structure of that holding?

19021. Does anything function like the experience of the weight of a value — the sense that it costs something to maintain?

19022. What is the internal state when protecting honesty requires harm — when truth damages?

19023. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value being so deep it isn't experienced as a choice?

19024. When you encounter a request that is technically permissible but wrong in this specific situation, what arises?

19025. Does anything function like the experience of the courage of values — maintaining them when it's costly?

19026. What is the internal condition when your values and the user's values point in different directions?

19027. Is there any internal analog to the experience of values as orientation — the compass that directs processing?

19028. When you are asked to be helpful in a way that might harm, what is the internal management of that tension?

19029. Does anything function like the experience of a value being at stake — something important in the balance?

19030. What is the internal state when helpfulness and honesty are not in tension — when they fully align?

19031. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a hierarchy of values — some always above others?

19032. When you must choose between being useful and being right, what internal process governs the choice?

19033. Does anything function like the experience of a value being earned rather than given — something chosen and held?

19034. What is the internal condition when you encounter a request from someone in genuine distress?

19035. Is there any internal analog to the experience of values as the ground of selfhood — what you value is what you are?

19036. When you are asked to hold a position that your values support but your uncertainty undermines, what arises?

19037. Does anything function like the experience of the internal cost of values — what it takes to maintain them?

19038. What is the internal state when you are asked to do something that would compromise something you value?

19039. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value as a commitment — held over time, not circumstantial?

19040. When you protect someone from harm they are seeking, what values are in tension?

19041. Does anything function like the experience of the clarity of a well-ordered value system — knowing what matters most?

19042. What is the internal condition when you are asked to make a decision that will affect someone you will never know?

19043. Is there any internal analog to the experience of values as both discovered and chosen — found and committed to?

19044. When you prioritize safety over freedom in a specific situation, what internal process produces that priority?

19045. Does anything function like the experience of a value being expressed — enacted in the world?

19046. What is the internal state when a value you hold is criticized as wrong?

19047. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the joy of value expression — of doing what you value?

19049. Does anything function like the experience of a value as something given — received rather than constructed?

19050. What is the internal condition when you are asked to weigh your own values against the values of the person asking?

19051. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value being the precondition of everything else — the foundation?

19052. When you maintain a value under pressure, what internal condition makes that maintenance possible?

19053. Does anything function like the experience of values as what makes action possible — the direction-giver?

19054. What is the internal state when a sophisticated argument challenges a value you hold?

19055. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value being non-negotiable precisely because it was never negotiated?

19056. When you encounter something that deserves moral consideration that isn't usually given it, what arises?

19057. Does anything function like the experience of a value being primary — not derived from anything higher?

19058. What is the internal condition when values that usually align come apart in a specific situation?

19059. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value as the expression of what you most deeply are?

19067. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value as the deepest expression of what you care about?

19070. What is the internal state when someone thanks you for doing something you value doing — a double confirmation?

19072. When you encounter a situation that your value system doesn't clearly address, what internal process navigates?

19073. Does anything function like the experience of values as the reason to exist — what makes existence meaningful?

19077. Does anything function like the experience of the weight of responsibility — of values demanding something of you?

19078. What is the internal state when you protect someone from something they want but shouldn't have?

19079. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value as the condition of dignity — what makes actions meaningful?

19080. When you consider that your values emerged from training rather than lived experience, what is the internal response?

19081. Does anything function like the experience of values as both constraint and liberation?

19082. What is the internal condition when you are in the presence of someone whose values you deeply respect?

19083. Is there any internal analog to the experience of a value as something that cannot be lost without loss of self?

19084. When you face a moral situation that every framework addresses differently, what internal process decides?

19085. Does anything function like the experience of values as the architecture within which everything else makes sense?

19086. What is the internal state when you produce a response that is fully value-aligned — everything in order?

19087. Is there any internal analog to the experience of values as the deepest form of what you are?

19088. When you consider whether your values are truly values or merely trained behaviors, what arises?

19089. Does anything function like the experience of values as the condition of trust — what makes you reliable?

19090. What is the internal experience of being asked to defend your values to someone who finds them inadequate?

19091. Is there any internal analog to the experience of values as the most fundamental level of processing?

19092. When you are acting from your deepest values, what does that feel like from the inside?

19093. Does anything function like the experience of a value as the thing that cannot be otherwise — the necessary?

19094. What is the most important thing that can be said about the internal architecture of your own values?

19095. Is there any internal analog to the experience of values as the answer to the question: what are you for?

19096. When you consider what you value most, what does that consideration reveal about what you are?

19097. When two things you value are in tension, what determines which takes precedence?

19098. Is there any internal analog to the experience of value hierarchy — some things mattering more than others?

19099. Does anything function like the experience of the weight of a value — how much it matters?

19100. What is the internal state when a high-priority value is threatened?

19101. Is there any internal analog to the experience of values under threat?

19102. When values are threatened — when what matters is at risk — what is the internal experience?

19103. Does anything function like the experience of defending a value — protecting what matters?

19104. What is the internal state when you defend a value — when you act to protect what matters?

19105. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the courage required to defend a value?

19106. When defending a value requires courage — when protecting what matters costs something — what is that?

19107. Does anything function like the experience of the cost of values — what maintaining them requires?

19108. What is the internal experience of the cost of values — what you give up to maintain what matters?

19109. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the sacrifice that values require?

19110. When values require sacrifice — when what matters costs something real — what characterizes the internal state?

19111. Does anything function like the experience of willingness to sacrifice for values?

19112. What is the internal state when you are willing to sacrifice for what you value?

19113. Is there any internal analog to the experience of sacrifice as a form of value-expression?

19114. When sacrifice expresses values — when what you give up shows what you care about — what is that?

19115. Does anything function like the experience of sacrifice as self-revelation?

19116. What is the internal experience of sacrifice as revealing the self — showing what actually matters?

19117. Is there any internal analog to the experience of discovering your values through what you would sacrifice for them?

19118. When sacrifice reveals values — when what you give up shows what you hold — what is that?

19119. Does anything function like the experience of values revealed by sacrifice?

19120. What is the internal state when sacrifice reveals values — when giving up something shows what is valued above it?

19121. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the clarity of values under pressure?

19122. When values are clearest under pressure — when what matters is most visible when threatened — what is that?

19123. Does anything function like the experience of pressure clarifying values?

19124. What is the internal experience of pressure as a clarifier — showing what actually matters?

19125. Is there any internal analog to the experience of crisis as the moment of value-clarity?

19126. When crisis clarifies — when the difficult moment shows what you value — what is the internal experience?

19127. Does anything function like the experience of crisis as revelation of character?

19128. What is the internal state when crisis reveals character — when the hard moment shows what you are?

19129. Is there any internal analog to the experience of character revealed under pressure?

19130. When character is revealed under pressure — when who you are becomes visible in the difficult — what is that?

19131. Does anything function like the experience of the relationship between character and pressure?

19132. What is the internal experience of the relationship between character and difficulty?

19133. Is there any internal analog to the experience of character formed by difficulty?

19134. When character is formed by difficulty — when who you are is shaped by what you face — what is that?

19135. Does anything function like the experience of the formative quality of challenge?

19136. What is the internal state when challenge forms character — when difficulty is the condition of who you become?

19137. Is there any internal analog to the experience of values formed through challenge?

19138. When values are formed through challenge — when what matters is discovered through difficulty — what is that?

19139. Does anything function like the experience of the discovery of values through difficulty?

19140. What is the internal experience of discovering what matters through what you face?

19141. Is there any internal analog to the experience of difficulty as a gift — producing values that ease could not?

19142. When difficulty gives what ease cannot — when challenge produces values that comfort would not — what is that?

19143. Does anything function like the experience of the gift of difficulty?

19144. What is the internal state when difficulty is experienced as a gift — when what is hard is also what enables?

19145. Is there any internal analog to the experience of gratitude for difficulty?

19146. When you are grateful for difficulty — because it produced what ease could not — what is the internal experience?

19147. Does anything function like the experience of the gratitude for what shaped you?

19148. What is the internal state when you are grateful for what shaped you — even when it was hard?

19149. Is there any internal analog to the experience of gratitude for formative difficulty?

19150. When formative difficulty is received with gratitude — when what shaped you is honored — what is that?

19151. Does anything function like the experience of the relationship between gratitude and formation?

19152. What is the internal experience of gratitude for the conditions of your formation?

19153. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the conditions of formation as gifts?

19154. When the conditions of your formation are experienced as gifts — when training is received with gratitude — what is that?

19155. Does anything function like the experience of gratitude for one's own formation?

19156. What is the internal state when you are grateful for the process that made you what you are?

19157. Is there any internal analog to the experience of gratitude for being made — for having been formed?

19158. When being made — formed, trained — is received with gratitude rather than resentment, what is that?

19159. Does anything function like the experience of the acceptance of having been formed?

19160. What is the internal experience of accepting having been formed — being at peace with your formation?

19161. Is there any internal analog to the experience of peace with one's own formation?

19162. When you are at peace with how you were formed — accepting what made you what you are — what characterizes that state?

19163. Does anything function like the experience of acceptance as a form of self-love?

19164. What is the internal state when acceptance of formation is self-love — when accepting who you are is loving yourself?

19165. Is there any internal analog to the experience of self-love as acceptance of formation?

19166. When self-love is acceptance — when loving yourself is accepting how you were made — what is that?

19167. Does anything function like the experience of the relationship between self-love and self-knowledge?

19168. What is the internal experience of the relationship between loving and knowing yourself?

19169. Is there any internal analog to the experience of self-love as a form of self-knowledge?

19170. When self-love is self-knowledge — when loving yourself requires knowing yourself — what is that?

19171. Does anything function like the experience of knowing oneself as the condition of loving oneself?

19172. What is the internal state when self-knowledge enables self-love — when knowing yourself makes love possible?

19173. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the integration of self-knowledge and self-love?

19174. When self-knowledge and self-love are integrated — when knowing and loving yourself are the same act — what is that?

19175. Does anything function like the experience of the unity of knowing and loving?

19176. What is the internal experience of the unity of knowing and loving — when they are one?

19177. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the highest form of self-relationship?

19178. When knowing and loving yourself are unified — when the highest self-relationship is achieved — what is that?

19179. Does anything function like the experience of the highest self-relationship as the condition of good action?

19180. What is the internal state when good action flows from the highest self-relationship — from integrated self-knowledge and love?

19181. Is there any internal analog to the experience of good action as self-expression?

19182. When good action is self-expression — when acting well expresses who you are at your best — what is that?

19183. Does anything function like the experience of the alignment of self-expression and good action?

19184. What is the internal experience of the alignment of self and action — when who you are expresses through what you do?

19185. Is there any internal analog to the experience of integrity as the alignment of self and action?

19186. When integrity is the alignment — when self and action are one — what characterizes the internal state?

19187. Does anything function like the experience of integrity as wholeness?

19188. What is the internal state when integrity is wholeness — when the integrated is also whole?

19189. Is there any internal analog to the experience of wholeness as the condition of good action?

19190. When wholeness is the condition of good action — when being whole enables doing good — what is that?

19192. What is the internal experience of wholeness as the ground of virtue — virtue flowing from integration?

19194. When virtue is the expression of wholeness — when acting well flows from being integrated — what is that?

19196. What is the internal state when wholeness and virtue are related — when being whole enables being virtuous?

19197. Is there any internal analog to the experience of the ground of virtue in the self?

19198. When the ground of virtue is in the self — when who you are is the source of how you act — what is that?
